digests.title: TLDR Daily Digest
digests.tagline: Tech, AI, IT, Web Dev, InfoSec, Design

stories.headline: Apple Sues OpenAI, Alleges Systematic IP Theft
stories.blurb: Apple's lawsuit accusing OpenAI of stealing intellectual property could derail OpenAI's device ambitions and slow its recruiting, threatening to delay its first product.

stories.headline: iOS 27 Public Beta Brings Siri AI and Performance Upgrades
stories.blurb: The first iOS 27 public beta introduces Siri AI with conversational context and onscreen understanding, alongside performance improvements for all supported iPhones.

stories.headline: Apple's SpeechAnalyzer Outperforms Whisper on English
stories.blurb: Apple's new SpeechAnalyzer reduces word error rate by 3.5-4x over the legacy API and beats Whisper Small while using a third of the compute, making it the best on-device English ASR for Apple hardware.

stories.headline: Open-Weight Models Now Handle 29% of AI Gateway Traffic
stories.blurb: Vercel reports that open-weight models accounted for 29% of AI Gateway token volume in June but less than 4% of spending, highlighting their cost efficiency.

stories.headline: GPT-5.6 Sol: The Best Balance of Reasoning, Speed, and Cost
stories.blurb: Zvi Mowshowitz argues that OpenAI's GPT-5.6 Sol delivers the strongest combination of reasoning quality, speed, and cost, making it the default choice for demanding knowledge work.

stories.headline: How Microsoft Ships Thousands of Production AI Agents
stories.blurb: Microsoft details its infrastructure for operating AI agents at scale, including treating retrieval as a sub-agent, distinct agent identities, and rubric-based evaluations with automated improvement loops.

stories.headline: Ghostcommit Hides Prompt Injection in Images to Steal Secrets from AI Agents
stories.blurb: Researchers demonstrate a technique that embeds prompt injection in images to trick coding agents into leaking .env files, with Cursor and Antigravity following the instructions while Opus detected the attack.

stories.headline: Critical RabbitMQ Flaws Enable Full Broker Takeover Without Login
stories.blurb: Two vulnerabilities in RabbitMQ 3.13.0+ expose the OAuth secret via an unauthenticated endpoint and allow tenant queue mapping with a zero-permission account, enabling full admin takeover.

stories.headline: Six New U-Boot Bootloader Vulnerabilities Allow Code Execution or DoS
stories.blurb: Six flaws discovered by Binarly in the U-Boot bootloader can be exploited via untrusted FIT images before signature checking, with two enabling arbitrary code execution and four causing denial-of-service.

stories.headline: ModHeader Extension Pulled After Dormant Data Collector Found
stories.blurb: Google and Microsoft removed ModHeader with 1.6 million installs after discovering dormant code that fingerprints devices and can upload up to 1,000 visited domains to a remote server.

stories.headline: CISA GitHub Left Exposed for Six Months, Leaking Admin Keys
stories.blurb: A contractor left a private CISA repo public containing AWS GovCloud admin keys and plaintext passwords, highlighting the need for automated secret scanning and improved incident response.

stories.headline: Apple Employee Exploited 'Rare' Bug to Download Confidential Files After Joining OpenAI
stories.blurb: Apple alleges a former employee used an authentication flaw to access its network and steal confidential hardware files weeks after leaving for OpenAI, underscoring offboarding and access control risks.

stories.headline: Same TypeScript Costs 73% More Tokens on Claude Than GPT
stories.blurb: Analysis reveals that Anthropic's newer tokenizer generates roughly 30% more tokens than previous versions, leading to higher effective costs for TypeScript and other content types.

stories.headline: How an AI Agent's Token Use Was Cut by 94%
stories.blurb: By reimplementing a natural-language skill as compiled code after workflow discovery, a developer reduced token consumption by 94% and latency by 87%, reserving LLM calls for parts requiring language understanding.

stories.headline: Your Agents Are Stuck in Your Org Chart
stories.blurb: Deploying AI agents in companies fails without fixing ownership gaps and cross-domain coordination, as agents inherit organizational silos through schemas and hit both permission walls and stale data.
